properties: Anotea flavida is also known as New Zealand pepper tree or yellow anise tree. It has been traditionally used in Māori medicine to treat a variety of ailments. intro: Anotea flavida, also known as Anethum graveolens, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the Middle East and the Mediterranean region. The health benefits of Anotea flavida include its anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which make it useful in treating digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include carvone, limonene, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Anotea flavida is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tea, essential oil, and infusions, which are often used to relieve digestive discomfort, promote relaxation, and reduce stress.
